title: pi-context update reconciles user schema edits by the hybrid route (deterministic merge + resolver on conflicts)
context: FGAP-046: install --update overwrites a user's edits to a catalog-shipped schema (resyncSchema, index.ts:464-583), and the 3-way merge base is not retained — checkStatus keeps a SHA-256 of the as-installed schema, not the body (context.ts:79). FEAT-006 must preserve customizations on update. Two candidate routes (FGAP-046 proposed_resolution): a deterministic 3-way merge, or agent-mediated reconciliation in pi. Reframed and grounded in R-0008.
decision: On a locally-modified / both-diverged schema (per checkStatus), pi-context update runs a deterministic key/path-level draft-07 3-way merge (base = the base-stamped as-installed body; ours = installed; theirs = catalog) and auto-applies the merged schema when the typed conflict set is empty. On a non-empty conflict set it does NOT overwrite: it returns the typed conflict set — a rendered report plus the structured conflicts (path / base / ours / theirs). Because pi-context update is an agent-invoked CLI surface (run by Claude Code or another shell-LLM, which already holds the conflict set and the operator's authority and is interactive), the reconciler is the CALLING agent: it reconciles and applies the result via the existing write-schema auth-gate under the operator's confirm. update does NOT spawn a subordinate agent to reconcile. Update never silently clobbers a customization and never hard-blocks the remaining schemas.
consequences: Decomposes into ordered, independently-shippable slices: base-stamping (store the as-installed schema body via the content-addressed object store) -> deterministic schema-merge (auto-apply clean, emit a typed conflict set) -> conflict resolution (return the typed conflict set to the calling agent + a rendered report). The user gets 'never silently clobbered' first, 'smart conflict help' via the calling agent.
Closes FGAP-046 (preserve schema customizations) + FGAP-049 (consult checkStatus before overwrite) across the base-stamping + merge + resolver tasks.

The merge engine is a new structure-aware key/path-level 3-way primitive: FEAT-002 (the substrate git merge driver) is proposed/unbuilt and DEC-0004 §6 names but does not author the schema-merge strategy; the primitive is designed so FEAT-002 later reuses the framework via its git %O base. FGAP-046 does not depend on FEAT-002 shipping.
Per correctness-over-cost: the hybrid is chosen on user-correctness; its higher build cost is sequencing (base-stamping -> merge -> resolver), not a tradeoff against correctness.

Resolver-route correction (FGAP-068): the resolver returns the conflict to the CALLING agent rather than spawning a subordinate pi-bound mergetool. The original subordinate-spawn route used `pi -p`, which runs the spawned session non-interactively (hasUI=false) so its write-schema was auth-gate-refused — the wrong abstraction for an agent-invoked CLI where the caller is already the interactive, credentialed reconciler. Surfaced by the live credentialed smoke (the structural fake-spawn verification missed it).

options_considered.label: R1 — deterministic 3-way merge only
options_considered.description: Key/path-level draft-07 3-way merge; auto-merge disjoint edits; surface every semantic conflict as a typed report for the user to resolve.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Deterministic + offline; punts rename / type-change / overlapping-edit conflicts to the user.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Hands a JSON-Schema 3-way conflict to the non-developer FGAP-046 exists to protect — not done-correctly for the target user.

options_considered.label: R2 — agent-mediated reconciliation only
options_considered.description: Route every locally-modified schema through a pi-bound agent that reconciles by judgement, gated by dry-run + auth-gate.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Lowest build (reuses pi-bound + write-schema + auth-gate); resolves semantics; but a credentialed pi call + interactive confirm on every modified schema, and cannot run unattended.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Taxes routine disjoint updates with a model call + prompt and hard-blocks unattended/CI updates (the auth-gate refuses non-interactive) — not done-correctly.

options_considered.label: Hybrid — deterministic merge + resolver on conflicts
options_considered.description: Auto-merge clean/disjoint edits silently; route only the typed conflict set to an agent mergetool (or a conflict report when non-interactive).
options_considered.tradeoffs: Best coverage: silent for the common case, intelligent help for the rare conflict, never destructive, never hard-blocked; cost = the merge engine + a cheap resolver seam.

references.label: resyncSchema overwrite; base body not retained
